title: quercus mcvaughii
intro: Quercus mcvaughii, also known as the McVaugh oak, is a species of deciduous tree native to the mountains of Mexico. The bark, leaves, and roots of Quercus mcvaughii have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Quercus mcvaughii include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting digestive health. Quercus mcvaughii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
